description: In C, run the package-local gate, fix the first owned diagnostic, rerun, then broaden.
body: In C, the fastest path through errors is to let cc -Wall -Wextra -Werror with the same standard (-std=c11) the build uses and clang-tidy, cppcheck, and -fanalyzer warnings drive one fix at a time rather than guessing. Use when make / Makefile targets (and the exact CC, CFLAGS, and -I/-L flags) or the analyzers report multiple diagnostics. Do not batch speculative edits across files, and avoid suppressing diagnostics to move on. Actions: inspect the package, run the gate, fix only the first compiler error with file:line and the caret-marked token, rerun the unit binary plus valgrind on the changed translation unit, then widen to make / Makefile targets (and the exact CC, CFLAGS, and -I/-L flags) for the whole package. Validation signals include a shrinking diagnostic count, a passing focused test, and a clean package-wide gate. Risk boundary: if a diagnostic implies a design or API change, stop and escalate instead of forcing the gate green.
